strengths: Large latency reduction (88.9%, effect_size=8.97x) is convincing and well-confirmed at seq_len=512.
Memory reduction from 6.32MB to 0.68MB (5.6x) adds practical value beyond speed.
Pipeline executed end-to-end with bundle ready; experimental methodology is reproducible.
weaknesses: The core prediction — throughput scales linearly up to 32k tokens — was never tested. All results are at seq_len=512, three orders of magnitude below the claimed regime.

Approximation error is severe: relative L2 error of 0.767 with verdict 'inconclusive'. The paper's own problem_statement acknowledges existing linear approximations 'lose accuracy' — this result suggests the proposed method has the same flaw.
No downstream task accuracy reported. Speed without quality is not a contribution; the accuracy-speed tradeoff is uncharacterized.
The 'chunked recurrence' mechanism from the title is not evidenced in any claim or ablation — unclear if it was even implemented.
Memory claim confidence is only 0.65, and no scaling behavior of memory is shown across sequence lengths.
required_revisions: Run the scaling sweep to 32k tokens and demonstrate linear throughput scaling — this is the single required prediction and it is entirely untested.
Ablate the chunked recurrence mechanism specifically: show its contribution to both speed and approximation quality versus naive linear attention.
Characterize the accuracy-speed tradeoff: report perplexity or downstream task metrics at multiple sequence lengths, not just L2 approximation error.
Reduce approximation error below 0.5 relative L2 or provide theoretical/empirical justification for why 0.767 is acceptable in practice.
Report results with p-values or confidence intervals across multiple random seeds — all three claims currently lack p_value.

evidence_blockers.reason: Listed in evidence_plan_keys but no scaling results beyond seq_len=512 appear in claims. The required prediction (linear scaling to 32k) is completely untested.
Listed in evidence_plan_keys but no ablation results appear in experimental_claims. Chunked recurrence contribution is unknown.

Listed in evidence_plan_keys but insight falsification is null and no falsification evidence is presented. The high approximation error (0.767) could itself be a falsification signal that was not addressed.
The single required prediction (linear throughput scaling to 32k tokens) has no corresponding experimental claim.
